author: Sofia Martínez
title: How Solar Installers Win Leads With Google Local Services Ads
description: Solar companies spend 3x on ads but convert half as many leads. Here's how to fix your targeting, messaging, and follow-up.

sections.p: We work with 12+ solar installation companies across the Southwest, and we see the same pattern every time: they're bidding on broad keywords, attracting tire-kickers, and wondering why their cost-per-lead keeps climbing. The reality is that solar installation is a high-intent, high-ticket purchase—your marketing should reflect that. Google Local Services Ads (LSA) consistently outperforms traditional search ads for solar because it filters for qualified, ready-to-act homeowners. But only if you set it up right.

sections.h2: Why Google Local Services Ads Win for Solar

sections.p: Solar installers using LSA see 35-50% lower cost-per-lead compared to regular Google Ads, according to our account data. Here's why: LSA shows your business prominently to people searching "solar installation near me" who've already decided they're serious about it. No ad copy, no landing pages to build—just your company name, ratings, and license status. Google handles pre-qualification. You only pay when someone books a consultation or calls you directly.

The second advantage is trust signals. Homeowners see your Google reviews, your service area, and (critically) that you're verified and licensed by Google. In a space where trust is everything, that's massive. We've seen solar companies' phone inquiry volume jump 2-3x in the first 60 days after launching LSA, even with modest budgets ($800-1,500/month).

sections.h2: Set Your LSA Budget and Bid Strategy

sections.p: Don't start with a huge budget. We recommend $1,000-1,500/month for your first two months, then scale based on conversion data. LSA doesn't work like traditional ads where you bid on keywords—instead, you set a max lead fee and Google controls when your ad appears. For solar, we're seeing competitive max lead fees of $35-65 depending on your location and seasonality. Summer months (March-August) are 2-3x pricier than winter because demand spikes.

list: Track phone leads separately from online booking leads (phone leads convert 20-40% higher)
Set your service radius realistically—don't pay for leads 45+ minutes away
Enable lead notifications so you call back within 2 hours (response time matters)
Verify your installer license and insurance on your profile weekly (Google flags outdated credentials)
Set up conversion tracking in Google Ads to see which LSA leads actually close

sections.h2: Complement LSA With Organic Local SEO

sections.p: LSA gets you immediate visibility, but it's not sustainable as your only channel. Build parallel organic visibility in local search. This means optimizing your Google Business Profile (GBP) with high-quality photos of actual installations, detailed service descriptions, and regular posts about seasonal maintenance. We've seen solar companies' organic lead volume increase 40-60% over 6 months by consistently updating their GBP with project photos and seasonal content.

Create location-specific landing pages for the cities and towns you serve. Don't just copy-paste the same page with different city names—actually address local considerations. "Solar installation in Tucson" has different permitting timelines and sun angles than "solar installation in Denver." Your content should reflect that specificity. Link from your location pages to relevant blog posts (ROI calculators, local incentive guides, system maintenance tips).

sections.quote: LSA gets you leads now. SEO gets you leads 6 months from now without paying per lead. You need both working together.

sections.h2: Convert Leads With Fast Follow-Up and Clear ROI

sections.p: Your ad spend means nothing if you can't convert. Solar leads are timing-sensitive—homeowners are researching and comparing multiple quotes simultaneously. You have 4 hours maximum to call back or you're competing with 2-3 other solar companies. Set up your CRM to automatically send a text message within 15 minutes of lead capture, regardless of whether a salesperson picks up the phone. We've seen this single change increase consultation booking rates by 25%.

On every sales call, quantify the ROI. Don't just talk about kWh and panel efficiency—lead with dollars saved. "Based on your average monthly bill of $140 and our installation at $18,000, you'll break even in 8 years and save $32,000 over 25 years." Homeowners respond to specific numbers, not vague promises about "going green." Your sales script should include 2-3 ROI scenarios based on system size and local electricity rates.
